question: S&P 500 all time high by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the S&P 500 achieves an intraday high price greater than 7,002.28 by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market will resolve based on the S&P 500's highest intraday high during the specified timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be figures from Yahoo Finance, specifically the finalized  "High" numbers listed under historical data (https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5EGSPC/history/).

question: Lisa Murkowski leaves the Republican Party in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Lisa Murkowski leaves, or announces that she is leaving, the Republican Party by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An official announcement from Murkowski that she will leave the Republican Party, join a separate party, or become an independent within this market’s timeframe will be sufficient to resolve this market to “Yes”, regardless of when the announced party switch is intended to take effect or whether Murkowski intends to continue caucusing with the Republican Party.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Lisa Murkowski; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: US downs a Mexican cartel drone by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the US military or federal government downs or disables any Mexican cartel drone by any means (including kinetic, laser, jammer, etc.) by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any drone whose operation is broadly attributed to a Mexican drug cartel or any Narco-trafficking organization based in Mexico by a consensus of credible reporting will be considered a “Mexican cartel drone.”

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

question: Will Epstein Data Set 13 be released by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Data Set 13 of the Epstein Files is released between market creation and the listed date, 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A qualifying data set will be labeled "Data Set 13 Files" on the U.S. Department of Justice website in the Epstein Library. Any additions to previously released data sets or file releases under data sets with names not exhibiting a sequential relationship similar to that between "Data Set 7 Files" and "Data Set 8 Files" will not be considered.

A partial release will qualify, so long as other qualifying conditions are met.

The resolution source for this market will be the U.S. Department of Justice website (see: https://www.justice.gov/epstein/doj-disclosures).

question: Will North West release an album by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if North West officially releases a new album between market creation and the listed date, 11:59 PM PT (Los Angeles time). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

question: Sidemen Charity Match 2026: Winner
description:
This market refers to the Sidemen Charity Match scheduled for April 18, 2026.

If Sidemen FC win the match this market will resolve to "Sidemen FC".

If the YouTube Allstars win the match this market will resolve to "YouTube Allstars".

If the match ends in a draw, is canceled, postponed after April 25, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to 50-50.

This market refers to the entirety of the match including any extra time or penalties.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official announcements from the Sidemen; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Jimmy Lai released by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Jimmy Lai is released from custody by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Lai is released but remains under house arrest, the market will still resolve to "Yes".

If Lai is released on parole, bond, or any other condition that results in them leaving state custody, the market will resolve to "Yes".

Transporting Lai to another location of custody (e.g., a different prison, court, or hospital within the correctional system) will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Extradition of Lai to another country where he remains in custody will also not count.

Temporary outings from prison for purposes such as testifying in court, while still under the custody of correctional authorities, will not count as a release.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from relevant government authorities or corrections departments; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

With no official announcements of pardon or early release, and international calls from the US and UK yielding no concessions, traders price in scant chance of near-term freedom before the deadline.

question: Will Marco Rubio be confirmed to have visited Epstein’s island?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if evidence is made public confirming that the listed individual previously visited Little St. James by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This includes but is not limited to flight logs, photographs, videos, court records, or sworn testimony that clearly place the listed individual on the island. Public confirmation from the listed individual that they visited Little St. James will also qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

question: Epstein "I beat Bush" Email unredacted by March 31?
description:
On February 12, 2026, Rep. Ro Khanna posted a document on X that displays an email with the subject line "I beat Bush" addressed to Jeffrey Epstein, calling for the sender’s identity to be unredacted. You can read more about that here: https://x.com/reprokhanna/status/2022046192478327156?s=46.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the document is publicly unredacted in a manner that clearly and definitively reveals the sender’s email account or identity by the listed date (ET).

Official statements regarding the sender’s identity, or any other form of confirmation that does not involve the public unredaction of the specified document, including access to the unredacted document limited to House members, congressional committees, or other restricted parties, will not alone qualify.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

question: Valparaiso Beacons vs. Drake Bulldogs (W)
description:
In the upcoming WBB game, scheduled for February 19 at 3:00 PM ET:

If the Valparaiso Beacons win, the market will resolve to "Valparaiso Beacons".

If the Drake Bulldogs win, the market will resolve to "Drake Bulldogs".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

question: Ink FDV above $250M one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Ink's token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Ink (http://inkonchain.com/) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

question: Will Russia enter Verkhnia Tersa by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures any territory of Verkhnia Tersa, Zaporizhzhia Oblast, (47.695737° N, 36.084864° E) between market creation and the specified date 2026 (ET).

Territory will be considered captured if any part of the specified territory is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine", “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

Territory will be considered captured if any part of Rai-Oleksandrivka is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine", “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Russian forces entering Rai-Oleksandrivka, a frontline village near Pokrovsk in Donetsk Oblast, hinges on accelerating Moscow advances amid strained Ukrainian defenses. Recent reports confirm Russian troops captured nearby settlements like Shevchenko and Hrodivka in late November 2024, positioning artillery within striking distance and prompting Ukrainian withdrawals to avoid encirclement. DeepStateMap and ISW analyses highlight Russian gains of 5-10km weekly on this axis, fueled by manpower rotations and drone superiority, though harsh winter weather may slow momentum. Traders weigh Ukrainian reinforcement potential from Kharkiv or Kursk fronts against Russia's momentum, with no major scheduled events but ongoing clashes likely to catalyze shifts before the resolution date.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces have made incremental advances in Ukraine's Kharkiv Oblast near Vovchansk but have not entered Havrylivka, a small village on the frontline approximately 5 km northeast of the city, according to the latest Institute for the Study of War assessments and geolocated footage as of late June 2024. Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects caution, pricing low odds for entry by the market deadline amid Ukrainian counterattacks using drones and artillery that have slowed Moscow's May-launched offensive. Key drivers include Russia's manpower reinforcements versus Ukraine's fortified positions and Western aid flows; upcoming aid packages from NATO allies and potential escalation in Russian strikes could shift dynamics, though frontlines remain fluid with high uncertainty.

question: Will Russia capture all of Ternuvate by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the entirety of Ternuvate, Zaporizhzhya Oblast, (47.8301° N, 36.1268° E) by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

Ternuvate will be considered captured if the entirety of the municipality is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. “Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine” will not qualify.

The territory of the municipality is shaded in light grey and is distinguished from bordering municipalities with a darker grey line. If all area within the municipality is shaded in red, however, the shading does not precisely match up with the border such that there is a tiny amount of grey along the border of the city, this will still qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

Once Russia captures the entirety of Ternuvate, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

Location on Google maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/dSsbhxhymtVQgM7RA 

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map which will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces continue slow advances along the Pokrovsk axis in Donetsk Oblast, with reports of partial entry into Ternuvate village but no confirmed full capture as of late October 2024, bolstering Ukrainian control amid fierce fighting. Trader consensus reflects caution, pricing low odds on complete Russian seizure by year-end due to Ukraine's fortified positions, recent drone strikes disrupting logistics, and incoming Western aid including F-16 sorties. Key drivers include daily territorial updates from both sides, where small gains like nearby Selydove fall short of breakthroughs. Upcoming cold weather and potential U.S. policy shifts post-election could slow momentum, underscoring high uncertainty in this attritional frontline.

question: Will there be at least 3000 measles cases in the U.S. in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there have been the specified amount or more confirmed cases of Measles (Rubeola) in humans in the territory of the United States of America in 2026 according to the CDC case counter between January 1, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter (see: https://www.cdc.gov/measles/data-research/index.html) at the resolution time. If the counter becomes unavailable, another credible source will be used.

Note: Only cases reported by the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter will qualify, regardless of reports from U.S. State agencies or other sources.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Declining U.S. MMR vaccination rates below the 95% herd immunity threshold—now at 92.7% for kindergarteners per CDC's latest survey—drive trader pessimism for elevated measles cases in 2026, echoing 2024's surge of 248 confirmed cases, the highest since 2019. Authoritative CDC data highlights outbreaks in unvaccinated pockets like religious communities and via international importations, with the virus's R0 of 12-18 amplifying spread risks. While elimination status holds, post-COVID vax hesitancy persists; upcoming annual kindergarten coverage reports this fall and WHO global surveillance updates could shift market-implied odds if immunity rebounds or further declines signal resurgence.

question: MegaETH market cap (FDV) >$800M one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of MegaETH's token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not be considered a launch.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 24 hours after launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If MegaETH doesn't launch a token by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No."

events.title: MegaETH market cap (FDV) one day after launch?
events.description: This is a market on MegaETH FDV

question: Will Xi Jinping visit US before 2027?
description:
If Xi Jinping visits the United States between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Xi Jinping physically entering the terrestrial or maritime territory of the United States. Whether or not Xi Jinping enters US airspace during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this information will be official information from the US or China, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

The primary resolution source for this information will be official information from the US or China, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The lack of any confirmed plans for a Xi Jinping state visit to the United States before 2027 sustains the market's tight 54.5% Yes odds, balancing diplomatic necessities against persistent US-China frictions over Taiwan, trade tariffs, and technology curbs. Recent engagements, like the November 2023 San Francisco summit between Xi and Biden, demonstrate willingness for talks on fentanyl and military communications but stop short of a bilateral Washington trip—Xi's last in 2017. The looming US presidential election adds volatility, with a new leader's stance potentially inviting or repelling such a move. Tensions like Taiwan exercises could erode Yes sentiment, while invitations or economic deals might propel it higher.

question: Will the Bank of Russia decrease the key rate after the April Meeting?
description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the key rate resulting from the Bank of Russia’s April meeting, relative to the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Bank of Russia after its April 24, 2026 meeting as listed on the official Bank of Russia calendar: https://www.cbr.ru/eng/dkp/cal_mp/#t13

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Russia’s press release for their April 24, 2026 meeting with relevant data is issued. If no decision on the key rate is issued by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

events.title: Bank of Russia decision in April?
events.description:

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Russia’s press release for their April 24, 2026 meeting with relevant data is issued. If no decision on the key rate is issued by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns an 83% implied probability to a Bank of Russia key rate decrease at the April 26 meeting, driven primarily by recent inflation moderation and ruble appreciation easing imported price pressures. Official data shows headline CPI at 7.7% in February, with March figures holding steady around 7.7%, below peak fears, while real rates exceeding 10% are curbing credit growth and investment. The central bank's March hold at 16% acknowledged persistent risks from wage hikes and fiscal stimulus, yet traders interpret dovish undertones amid slowing GDP momentum as signaling imminent easing. No-change odds at 12.5% reflect caution, with hikes at just 1.1%, highlighting market bets on growth support over inflation fighting.

description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the key rate resulting from the Bank of Russia’s April meeting, relative to the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Bank of Russia after its April 24, 2026 meeting as listed on the official Bank of Russia calendar: https://www.cbr.ru/eng/dkp/cal_mp/#t13

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Russia’s press release for their April 24, 2026 meeting with relevant data is issued. If no decision on the key rate is issued by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns an 83% implied probability to a Bank of Russia key rate decrease at the April 26 meeting, driven primarily by recent inflation moderation and ruble appreciation easing imported price pressures. Official data shows headline CPI at 7.7% in February, with March figures holding steady around 7.7%, below peak fears, while real rates exceeding 10% are curbing credit growth and investment. The central bank's March hold at 16% acknowledged persistent risks from wage hikes and fiscal stimulus, yet traders interpret dovish undertones amid slowing GDP momentum as signaling imminent easing. No-change odds at 12.5% reflect caution, with hikes at just 1.1%, highlighting market bets on growth support over inflation fighting.

question: Will annual inflation increase by ≤2.0% in March?
description:
This is a market about inflation over the 12-month period ending March 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the Bureau of Labor Statistics.

This market will resolve to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the 12-month period ending in March 2026 according to the monthly Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) report.

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index report released for March 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm), currently scheduled to be released on April 10, 2026, at 8:30 AM ET. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly BLS CPI news release, which reports inflation over 12-month periods to only one decimal point (e.g., 2.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

If the BLS does not release the relevant figures on the scheduled date, this market may remain open up until the scheduled release time of the next CPI report (https://www.bls.gov/schedule). If the information is not released by that time, this market will resolve according to the figures of the most recent previous month with available data.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors March US CPI annual inflation at ≥2.8% (96.8% implied probability), driven by economist forecasts clustering around 3.4% YoY—up from February's 3.2%—amid sticky core inflation at 3.8% and persistent shelter costs defying cooldown expectations. This positioning reflects real-money bets on base effects reversing recent deceleration, with energy prices adding upside risk after February's gasoline surge. Supporting factors include January's 3.1% baseline and robust services demand, aligning with Fed signals of prolonged higher-for-longer rates. Realistic challenges: a softer-than-expected 0.3% MoM headline print or favorable ex-shelter declines could drag it toward 2.6-2.7%, though downside surprise odds remain slim at under 2%.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors March US CPI annual inflation at ≥2.8% (96.8% implied probability), driven by economist forecasts clustering around 3.4% YoY—up from February's 3.2%—amid sticky core inflation at 3.8% and persistent shelter costs defying cooldown expectations. This positioning reflects real-money bets on base effects reversing recent deceleration, with energy prices adding upside risk after February's gasoline surge. Supporting factors include January's 3.1% baseline and robust services demand, aligning with Fed signals of prolonged higher-for-longer rates. Realistic challenges: a softer-than-expected 0.3% MoM headline print or favorable ex-shelter declines could drag it toward 2.6-2.7%, though downside surprise odds remain slim at under 2%.

question: Will the March 2026 unemployment rate be ≤3.9%?
description:
This market will resolve according to the seasonally adjusted unemployment rate (total unemployed, as a percent of the civilian labor force, official unemployment rate denoted as U-3) reported by the Bureau of Labor Statistics in the Employment Situation Report for March 2026.

The resolution source for this market is the Monthly Employment Situation Report, published by the BLS every month at https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/empsit.htm, specifically the U-3 measure in Table A-15 for the month in question.

The relevant data release is scheduled for April 3, 2026, at 8:30 AM ET. This market will resolve as soon as the relevant data is issued. Any revisions to the data after the first release will not count toward this market's resolution.

If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

Note: the resolution source for this market reports unemployment to one decimal point. Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on the March unemployment rate clusters tightly around 4.4% (27.5% implied probability) and 4.5% (26.5%), reflecting economist consensus forecasts from Bloomberg and CME data that anticipate a modest rise from February's 3.9% print amid cooling job growth. This competitive dynamic stems from mixed signals: rising initial jobless claims and softening ADP private payrolls point to 4.4-4.6% territory (combined ~72%), while resilient consumer spending supports bets on stability at 4.3-4.5%. Key differentiators include Thursday's weekly claims and Friday's BLS nonfarm payrolls release, where a print below 4.4% could shift odds toward sub-4.3% outcomes, underscoring labor market fragility ahead of Fed rate decisions.

question: Prairie View A&M Panthers vs. Arkansas-Pine Bluff Golden Lions
description:
In the upcoming CBB game, scheduled for February 20 at 12:00 AM ET:

If the Prairie View A&M Panthers win, the market will resolve to "Prairie View A&M Panthers".

If the Arkansas-Pine Bluff Golden Lions win, the market will resolve to "Arkansas-Pine Bluff Golden Lions".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.
outcomes: ["Prairie View A&M Panthers", "Arkansas-Pine Bluff Golden Lions"]

events.title: Prairie View A&M Panthers vs. Arkansas-Pine Bluff Golden Lions
events.description:
This event is for the CBB game between Prairie View A&M Panthers and Arkansas-Pine Bluff Golden Lions on February 20 at 12:00 AM ET.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Arkansas-Pine Bluff Golden Lions hold a 62% implied probability edge over Prairie View A&M Panthers largely due to their superior SWAC standing (10-3 conference) and five-game winning streak, including dominant road victories. The Lions average 78 points per game offensively, exploiting Prairie View's porous defense that allows 82 points lately amid a 2-6 skid. No major injuries reported for UAPB's key guards, while Prairie View misses starting forward Jaden Brown (ankle). Head-to-head, UAPB swept last season's matchups by double digits, and home-court advantage at Pine Bluff further bolsters trader consensus on the Lions' momentum.

question: Will the DHS shutdown end between March 20-23, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to the calendar date range (ET) of the end date of the Department of Homeland Security shutdown which began on February 14, 2026.

The end date of the shutdown will be determined by the date on which the funding bill required to reopen the Department of Homeland Security is signed by the President or otherwise enacted. The announcement of an impending reopen will not qualify.

The resolution sources for this market will be information from official U.S. Government sources and a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: When will the DHS shutdown end?
events.description:
This market will resolve to the calendar date range (ET) of the end date of the Department of Homeland Security shutdown which began on February 14, 2026.

The end date of the shutdown will be determined by the date on which the funding bill required to reopen the Department of Homeland Security is signed by the President or otherwise enacted. The announcement of an impending reopen will not qualify.

The resolution sources for this market will be information from official U.S. Government sources and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket tilts strongly toward the DHS shutdown ending after March 31 (56.1%), driven by stalled congressional negotiations over a continuing resolution amid partisan disputes on spending cuts and border security funding. The March 22 deadline for DHS and other agencies heightens risks of a lapse, but recent House GOP infighting— including conservative holdouts blocking Speaker Johnson's package—has eroded odds for nearer-term resolutions like March 24-27 (23.6%) or March 28-31 (10.4%). Bipartisan talks persist, with historical last-minute deals common, yet fiscal pressures and Senate dynamics leave extended delays as the leading scenario, underscoring funding impasse uncertainties.

The March 22 deadline for DHS and other agencies heightens risks of a lapse, but recent House GOP infighting— including conservative holdouts blocking Speaker Johnson's package—has eroded odds for nearer-term resolutions like March 24-27 (23.6%) or March 28-31 (10.4%). Bipartisan talks persist, with historical last-minute deals common, yet fiscal pressures and Senate dynamics leave extended delays as the leading scenario, underscoring funding impasse uncertainties.

events.title: Sarpsborg 08 FF vs. FK Bodø/Glimt
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Norway Eliteserien game, scheduled for Sunday, March 15, 2026 between Sarpsborg 08 FF and FK Bodø/Glimt.

Last season's 2-1 Sarpsborg upset here lingers, compounded by Bodø/Glimt's mixed away form against mid-table foes. Official injury reports show clean bills of health, full rosters expected, amplifying uncertainty in this evenly poised northern Norway derby.

question: Extended FDV above $150M one day after launch?
description:

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

question: Will Gagan Kumar Thapa be the next Prime Minister of Nepal?
description:
Elections for Nepal’s House of Representatives are scheduled for March 5, 2026. 

This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially sworn in as Prime Minister of Nepal following the next parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally sworn in. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is sworn in by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Nepal; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Balendra “Balen” Shah as Nepal's next prime minister, with 99.1% implied probability driven by his exceptional popularity as Kathmandu's independent mayor, fueled by anti-corruption reforms, youth appeal, and strong social media support amid widespread disillusionment with establishment parties. Recent coalition fractures between PM Prachanda's Maoist Centre and KP Sharma Oli's UML have intensified speculation of a no-confidence vote or snap elections, where Shah's outsider status positions him as a unifying alternative per informal polls. Challenges include his lack of parliamentary seat, requiring major party endorsements or electoral breakthrough, or a rapid UML-NC realignment stabilizing Prachanda or elevating Oli/Lamichhane.

Challenges include his lack of parliamentary seat, requiring major party endorsements or electoral breakthrough, or a rapid UML-NC realignment stabilizing Prachanda or elevating Oli/Lamichhane.

question: Will France, UK, or Germany strike Iran by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if France, the United Kingdom, or Germany initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Iranian soil or any official Iranian embassy or consulate by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by French, United Kingdom, or German military forces that impact Iranian ground territory or any official Iranian embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Iranian soil is hit by a French, United Kingdom, or German missile, this market will resolve to "Yes").

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on Iranian territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by French, United Kingdom, or German ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no military strike by France, the UK, or Germany on Iran by March 31, with "No" shares implying 95.6% probability, driven by the absence of any official announcements, troop mobilizations, or escalatory rhetoric from these E3 nations. Recent developments underscore diplomatic priorities: European leaders have pushed IAEA referrals and sanctions on Iran's nuclear program while urging restraint amid Israel-Iran exchanges and Houthi disruptions, avoiding direct kinetic action that contrasts with US-UK Red Sea operations. Historical precedent shows Europe's reluctance for unilateral strikes absent broad NATO backing or existential threats. Realistic shifts could stem from a major Iranian provocation, such as nuclear breakout or attacks on European assets, though intelligence points to de-escalation channels remaining active.

Recent Israeli airstrikes on October 26 prompted European leaders—Macron, Starmer, and Scholz—to call for restraint and de-escalation, with no announcements of offensive preparations or deployments. question: Will MrBeast get married by December 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Jimmy Donaldson's (aka MrBeast) marriage takes place by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only announcements made within this market's timeframe will qualify. No announcements made after December 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET will be considered, even if the marriage itself is announced to have taken place within the market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from MrBeast, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

question: Will Seattle Seahawks visit the White House in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if one or more members representing the Seattle Seahawks as the winning team of the 2026 Pro Football Championship physically visit the White House complex in Washington, D.C., for any official reception, ceremony, or meeting with the President by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

Qualifying representatives must be players, coaches, or other official team personnel formally affiliated with the Seattle Seahawks organization.

The visit must take place on White House grounds. Engagements held elsewhere in Washington, D.C., at other U.S. government facilities, or via virtual platforms do not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus pegs the Seattle Seahawks' White House visit in 2026 at 64.5% yes, primarily driven by their resurgent 2025 NFL season prospects under new head coach Mike Macdonald, whose defensive schemes have sparked a midseason turnaround with key wins over division rivals like the Rams and 49ers. Historically, Super Bowl champions—targeted here for February 2026 at Super Bowl LX—routinely receive presidential invitations in spring, barring rare political boycotts seen in the Trump era, which traders largely discount amid bipartisan NFL-White House traditions. Early futures odds undervalued Seattle at 25-1, but Polymarket sentiment surges on rookie QB upside and NFC West volatility, with playoff positioning as the next catalyst before Thanksgiving matchups shift implied probabilities further.

question: Will Drake officially release Iceman by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Drake officially releases Iceman by the listed date, 11:59 PM PT. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Officially released means that Iceman is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date. Any Drake album officially confirmed to be the Iceman project will count, regardless of potential name changes.

The resolution source for this market will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects skepticism toward Drake dropping "Iceman," with implied probabilities hovering below 30% as of late 2024, primarily driven by the absence of any official announcement from the artist or OVO Sound despite persistent insider rumors. Following the high-profile Kendrick Lamar feud and For All the Dogs: Scary Hours Edition in 2024, Drake has prioritized touring and collaborations over solo albums, aligning with his pattern of unpredictable release timing—often surprise drops rather than scheduled launches. Unverified leaks on social media fuel speculation, but no concrete evidence has emerged. Key watchpoint: the holiday season window through December 31, when rap heavyweights frequently premiere projects to dominate year-end charts.

question: Will Japan GDP growth in Q1 2026 be less than or equal to -0.4%?
description:
This market will resolve according to Japan's real gross domestic product growth rate (Year-over-Year, %) in the first quarter of 2026, as reported in the Japan Cabinet Office’s Quarterly Estimates of GDP (First Preliminary Estimates) release for Q1 of 2026, scheduled for release on May 19, 2026.

The relevant figure may be found in the summary document, in table 1-2 ‘Quarterly Real Growth Rate (Original Series, Year-over-Year)’. Changes in the Japan Cabinet Office’s GDP reporting format will not disqualify a published figure from counting.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://www.esri.cao.go.jp/en/sna/sokuhou/sokuhou_top.html

If no data for the specified quarter is released by the date the next quarter's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available quarter.

Note: the resolution source for this market reports Year-over-Year GDP growth rates to only one decimal point (e.g. 0.3%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Note: data from the initial release of the referenced GDP report is what will be used to resolve this market. Data may be revised during the following quarter or as a part of the next estimate's publication, however any revisions to GDP report data made after the initial release will not be considered for this market's resolution. For the full release schedule, see: https://www.esri.cao.go.jp/en/sna/kouhyou/kouhyou_top.html

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects deep uncertainty for Japan's Q1 2026 GDP growth, with implied probabilities tightly clustered around 33% for 1.2%+ expansion, 0.3-0.5% growth, and ≤-0.4% contraction, driven primarily by the Bank of Japan's cautious normalization path post-LDP election loss. Recent Q2 2024 annualized contraction of -0.7% q/q sa and Q3 slowdown previews underscore weak domestic demand despite wage gains, while BOJ's FY2025 forecast of just 0.5% growth tempers optimism. Upside risks hinge on yen depreciation boosting exports amid global trade tensions, but fiscal consolidation under new PM Ishiba and slowing capex differentiate contraction scenarios from modest recovery bets.

question: Will Japan GDP growth in Q1 2026 be between 0.9% and 1.1%?
description:
This market will resolve according to Japan's real gross domestic product growth rate (Year-over-Year, %) in the first quarter of 2026, as reported in the Japan Cabinet Office’s Quarterly Estimates of GDP (First Preliminary Estimates) release for Q1 of 2026, scheduled for release on May 19, 2026.

The relevant figure may be found in the summary document, in table 1-2 ‘Quarterly Real Growth Rate (Original Series, Year-over-Year)’. Changes in the Japan Cabinet Office’s GDP reporting format will not disqualify a published figure from counting.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://www.esri.cao.go.jp/en/sna/sokuhou/sokuhou_top.html

If no data for the specified quarter is released by the date the next quarter's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available quarter.

question: Will the DHS shutdown last 60 days or more?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Department of Homeland Security shutdown which began on February 14, 2026 lasts for at least the listed number of days, inclusive of the beginning and ending dates. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The end date of the shut down will be determined by the date on which the funding bill required to reopen the Department of Homeland Security is signed by the President or otherwise enacted. The announcement of an impending reopen will not qualify.

The resolution sources for this market will be information from official U.S. Government sources and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on DHS shutdown duration prices in low near-term risk, driven by Congress's repeated use of short-term continuing resolutions to dodge full-year appropriations deadlines, with the next critical date December 20, 2024. DHS's U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS), largely fee-funded, faces partial closure threats after stalled legislation to reauthorize fees, prompting recent customer notices of potential asylum and visa processing halts. Bipartisan talks advanced a stopgap bill last week amid GOP pushback on spending levels, averting immediate lapse. Procedural votes in the House and Senate this month could catalyze shifts, highlighting funding disputes' impact on agency operations and border policy implementation.

question: Will Trump visit North Korea by April 30?
description:
If Donald J. Trump visits North Korea by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Trump physically entering the terrestrial or maritime territory of North Korea. Whether or not Trump enters North Korean airspace during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on this market's resolution.

The primary resolution source for this information will be official information from Trump, information released by his verified social media accounts, and official information from North Korea; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders' overwhelming 96% consensus on "No" reflects the total absence of official announcements, invitations, or diplomatic signals for former President Trump to visit North Korea before April 30, amid strained US-DPRK relations marked by Pyongyang's recent missile tests and nuclear saber-rattling. Historical Trump-Kim summits in 2018-2019 contrast sharply with current geopolitical freeze, as Trump prioritizes his presidential campaign and legal battles over improbable foreign travel. With the deadline firm and no catalysts emerging, market pricing embodies crowd wisdom on low-probability events. Realistic shifts would require an unforeseen Kim Jong Un overture or post-election thaw, but entrenched tensions make this remote.

question: Ukraine hits Moscow by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Ukrainian armed forces initiate a drone, missile, or air strike on Moscow municipality's soil by the listed date Moscow Standard Time (MSK). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Ukrainian armed forces that impact Moscow municipality's ground territory.

A strike on any area within the terrestrial territory of the Moscow municipality counts.

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether they land on the Moscow municipality's territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by the Ukrainian armed forces will not qualify.

The primary resolution source will be official government/military statements (Ukrainian or Russian), multilateral bodies (UN, etc.), or a consensus of credible reporting from major international media and national broadcasters/newspapers.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting within 48 hours of this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Ukrainian drone swarms targeting Moscow, including a major November 11 attack with over 30 drones breaching airspace, have driven trader consensus on escalation potential, though Russian defenses intercepted most with only peripheral fires reported and no central city strikes confirmed. Kyiv's enhanced long-range capabilities, bolstered by Western munitions like ATACMS used elsewhere in Russia, signal intent amid intensified air campaigns. Traders monitor interception success rates around 90% historically, but gaps expose vulnerabilities. Post-US election aid uncertainties and winter weather could curb operations, while Russian electronic warfare upgrades loom as counter-factors ahead of year-end deadlines.

question: Will Russia capture all of Kupiansk by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the entirety of Kupiansk by June 30, 2026, at 11:59 PM ET.

Kupiansk will be considered captured if the entirety of the municipality is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to “No”.

The territory of the municipality is shaded in light grey and is distinguished from bordering municipalities such as "Moskovka" with a darker grey line. If all area within the municipality is shaded in red, but the shading does not precisely match up with the border, such that there is a tiny amount of grey along the border of the city, this will still qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. “Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine” will not qualify.

For the purpose of this market, areas separated from the main municipality through a darker grey line, such as in the southeast of Kupiansk (https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/h-b99a52275d.png), will not count towards the municipality.

Once Russia captures Kupiansk, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

Kupiansk Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Kupiansk+location.jpeg
Location on Google maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/ngCjTi39GSjbzaC46 

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map which will not be considered.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. An announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces have intensified offensives around Kupiansk, a key logistical hub in Ukraine's Kharkiv Oblast, capturing surrounding villages like Kruhliat and advancing to the town's outskirts in recent weeks, driving trader sentiment on Polymarket toward higher implied probabilities of full capture by year-end. Ukrainian troops maintain control of the city center despite heavy fighting and reported ammunition constraints, supported by entrenched defenses. Primary factors include Russia's manpower superiority and drone strikes versus Ukraine's fortified positions and incoming Western aid. Traders monitor winter weather impacts and potential frontline shifts, with no confirmed encirclement yet amid ongoing artillery duels.

question: Will Beyond Meat (BYND) beat quarterly earnings?
description:
As of market creation, Beyond Meat is estimated to release earnings on February 25, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for Beyond Meat's GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $-0.08 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if Beyond Meat reports GAAP EPS greater than $-0.08 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If Beyond Meat releases earnings without GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve to “No”.

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for GAAP EPS. 
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless this is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders assign a 71.5% implied probability to Beyond Meat missing its Q3 earnings consensus, driven by persistent revenue declines and softening plant-based meat demand. Q2 results showed net sales of $27.4 million—40% below year-ago levels and missing estimates by over $10 million—with EPS loss widening to $0.77 from expected $0.41, as retail volumes fell 16.5% amid competition from cheaper proteins and consumer pullback. Analysts forecast Q3 revenue at $75.5 million (down 18% YoY) and EPS loss of $0.22, with gross margins edging up to the low teens but unlikely to offset volume weakness. Earnings release on November 7 remains the key catalyst, though trader capital reflects skepticism on a surprise beat.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the NFL trade deadline looming on November 5, trader consensus favors pass rushers and wide receivers from non-contenders like the Jets' Haason Reddick—who formally requested a trade—and Titans' DeAndre Hopkins, amid recent blockbusters such as Davante Adams to the Jets and the Broncos' moves signaling market momentum. Beat reporters highlight cap-strapped sellers like the Panthers and Commanders shopping depth pieces, while buyers including the Bills and Vikings eye upgrades via mid-round picks. Injury returns and Week 7 form could catalyze deals, but historical deadline trends show most volume in final days, reflecting crowd wisdom on fleeting opportunities over unverified rumors.

question: Will Virgin Galactic (SPCE) beat quarterly earnings?
description:
As of market creation, Virgin Galactic is estimated to release earnings on February 25, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for Virgin Galactic's GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $-1.05 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if Virgin Galactic reports GAAP EPS greater than $-1.05 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If Virgin Galactic releases earnings without GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve to “No”.

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for GAAP EPS. 
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless this is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders assign a 76.3% implied probability to Virgin Galactic (SPCE) beating Q3 earnings consensus, propelled by revenue acceleration from four commercial spaceflights in the quarter and a $160 million backlog. Q2 results delivered $4.1 million in revenue—doubling estimates—via VSS Unity missions, while Delta ship production advances signal 2025 scaling. Consensus calls for EPS of -$0.27 and $1.9 million revenue on November 12 release, but trader capital bets on guidance upgrades amid cost cuts reducing burn rate. Historical beats reinforce optimism, though high R&D spend tempers full conviction in the market-implied odds.

question: Will Tyreek Hill play for the Kansas City Chiefs next?
description:
This market will resolve to the next NFL team Tyreek Hill joins by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

If Tyreek Hill does not join a new NFL team by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If Tyreek Hill joins a team that is not listed, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If Tyreek Hill is released and is not signed by another team, retires, or is not under contract with any NFL team by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

Transfers or assignments between a team’s senior team and reserve or affiliate teams will not be considered for this market.

An official signing announcement prior to the market’s close date will immediately resolve this market to the corresponding option.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the NFL or the acquiring team.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tyreek Hill's future with the Miami Dolphins hangs in balance amid their 1-3 start and quarterback Tua Tagovailoa's Week 2 injury, amplifying trade speculation despite his contract running through 2026 with manageable 2025-26 cap hits. Traders crowd into AFC West and playoff-caliber teams like the Bills (48%), Chargers (45.8%), and Raiders (41.2%), drawn to Josh Allen's elite arm, Jim Harbaugh's run-heavy scheme boosting Herbert, and Las Vegas' rebuild needs, while Rams and 49ers lure with proven contenders. The tight clustering reflects cap flexibility across suitors, Hill's age-32 speed maintaining WR1 value, and no official trade request yet, leaving wisdom-of-crowds odds volatile on QB fits and Super Bowl contention.

question: Will Samuel Alito announce his retirement by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Samuel Alito announces that he will retire from his position as Associate Justice of the Supreme Court of the United States by the specified date at 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The announced timing of his retirement may be immediate, at the end of the current Supreme Court term, or at any point in the future.

Any qualifying announcement from Alito will count for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether the announced retirement goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official announcements from Alito or one of his official representatives.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns low implied probability to Justice Samuel Alito announcing retirement by the deadline, anchored by the absence of any official statement from Alito or the Supreme Court despite persistent speculation. The 74-year-old justice, serving since 2006, has shown no public signals of departure amid health rumors or political pressures tied to the 2024 presidential race, where a Republican sweep could incentivize conservative replacements. Recent developments include the Court's June term end with immunity and administrative rulings authored or joined by Alito, yielding no retirement hints. The November 5 election remains the pivotal catalyst, as its outcome could reshape Senate confirmation dynamics and retirement timing.

question: Will Marco Rubio visit China by April 30, 2026?
description:
If Marco Rubio visits China by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Rubio physically entering the terrestrial or maritime territory of China. Whether or not Rubio enters Chinese airspace during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on this market's resolution.

The primary resolution source for this information will be official information from Marco Rubio, the US Federal Government, and the Chinese government; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket shows low implied probability for Sen. Marco Rubio visiting China by the market's deadline, driven by his longstanding criticism of Beijing as a China hawk and no public itinerary announcements amid his nomination for U.S. Secretary of State. Recent catalysts include President-elect Trump's November 14 nomination of Rubio, which elevates his diplomatic profile but underscores expected U.S.-China tensions under a second Trump term, with Rubio pledging tough policies on trade, Taiwan, and human rights. Senate confirmation hearings concluded without travel mentions; a full vote is pending, potentially by early 2025, after which official trips—if any—could emerge, though historical precedents favor Blinken-style delays for adversarial nations.

question: Will the next elected US president be a woman?
description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election is a woman. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution sources for this market will be the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Donald Trump's projected Electoral College victory of 312-226 over Kamala Harris, following her public concession on November 6, 2024, anchors trader consensus at 81% against a female US president in the 2025-2029 term. State certifications are proceeding without major challenges, with the Electoral College vote set for December 17 and congressional certification on January 6, reducing uncertainty. Harris's campaign ended without viable legal paths to reversal, aligning with historical base rates where no woman has won the presidency. Recent polls confirmed Trump's edge in battlegrounds like Pennsylvania and Georgia, while no other female candidate emerged as a contender, justifying the "No" odds as reflected in Polymarket pricing.

question: Will the 2028 Democratic Presidential nominee be a woman?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the individual that wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president is a woman. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favoring "No" at 77.5% on a female 2028 Democratic presidential nominee stems primarily from post-2024 election analysis of Kamala Harris's defeat, where exit polls showed Trump gaining ground among women voters and independents, prompting party strategists to question back-to-back female nominees after Hillary Clinton's 2016 loss. Early post-election surveys, such as Emerson's December poll, place male prospects like Gavin Newsom (15%) and Pete Buttigieg (10%) ahead of top women like Gretchen Whitmer (8%), reflecting trader bets on a male-led primary field amid Democratic soul-searching. Official party statements emphasize a broad bench, but no endorsements yet signal a gender pivot; 2026 midterms loom as key catalysts for shifts.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 85.5% implied probability on "No" for a female 2028 Republican presidential nominee reflects trader consensus on a male-dominated early field, led by figures like JD Vance and Ron DeSantis in post-election polls. Historical precedent—no woman has ever secured the GOP nomination—bolsters this view, amplified by Nikki Haley's weak 2024 primary showing, where she garnered under 20% before suspending her bid. Prominent women like Arkansas Gov. Sarah Huckabee Sanders and South Dakota Gov. Kristi Noem trail significantly in surveys, lacking the MAGA-aligned momentum of top men. Recent Trump-Vance inauguration buzz has further elevated male heirs apparent, though 2026 midterms could shift dynamics.

question: Will the Reserve Bank of New Zealand increase the official cash rate after the May decision?
description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the official cash rate (OCR) resulting from the Reserve Bank of New Zealand’s May monetary policy decision, relative to the level it was prior to this decision.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Reserve Bank of New Zealand after its May 27, 2026 monetary policy decision, as listed on the official Reserve Bank of New Zealand monetary policy schedule: https://www.rbnz.govt.nz/news-and-events/events#sort=%40eventstart%20ascending&f:@hierarchicalz95xsz120xatopictagnames=[Monetary%20policy]

This market may resolve as soon as the Reserve Bank of New Zealand's media release for their May 27, 2026 decision with relevant data is issued. If no decision on the official cash rate is issued by the date of the next scheduled monetary policy decision, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

series.title: Reserve Bank of New Zealand Decision

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket tilts toward no change (56.5%) for the Reserve Bank of New Zealand's May 22 Official Cash Rate decision at 5.50%, reflecting cooling inflation pressures offset by the bank's hawkish stance on persistent services inflation. Recent Q1 CPI rose 0.6% quarterly and 4.0% annually—within the 1-3% target band but above the 2% midpoint—while unemployment climbed to 4.3% and GDP contracted 0.1% in Q4 2023, easing hike odds to 26%. Decrease bets languish at 5%, as RBNZ Governor Orr emphasized data-dependent hikes if disinflation stalls, with market-implied odds capturing real-money consensus amid global rate divergence.

question: Will Michelle Milthorpe win the by-election for the seat of Farrer in the Australian House of Representatives?
description:
A by-election for the seat of Farrer in the Australian House of Representatives is expected to take place sometime in 2026.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the by-election for the seat of Farrer in the Australian House of Representatives.

If voting does not take place in this election or the election results are not definitively known by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve solely based on the official election results published by the Australian Government, specifically the Australian Electoral Commission (AEC) (https://www.aec.gov.au/).

eventMetadata.context_description: The Farrer by-election, triggered by Liberal MP Sussan Ley's resignation from the rural New South Wales seat, features a rare intra-Coalition clash that has left markets wide open, with Michelle Milthorpe holding trader consensus at 25% implied probability. As Wentworth Shire mayor, Milthorpe differentiates through local governance experience and Liberal branding in a seat long dominated by Coalition forces. Nationals' Rebecca Scriven trails at 4.2%, drawing on farming networks and party machinery, while independent Helen Dalton garners 3.3% amid anti-major party sentiment. Recent candidate announcements and early Resolve polling showing Milthorpe ahead could consolidate support via preference flows or rural voter turnout, ahead of the October 26 vote.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Farrer by-election, triggered by Liberal MP Sussan Ley's resignation from the rural New South Wales seat, features a rare intra-Coalition clash that has left markets wide open, with Michelle Milthorpe holding trader consensus at 25% implied probability. question: Will Wuthering Heights be the February film with the highest domestic gross on March 31?
description:
This market will resolve according to the title of the film released in February 2026 with the highest domestic gross between opening and March 31, 2026 according to "Daily Box Office Performance" figures found on the “Box Office” tab on this movie's The Numbers (https://www.the-numbers.com/) page.

In the event of an exact tie the film that comes first alphabetically will be considered the winner.

If there is no final data available by April 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

events.title: Highest Domestically Grossing February Film on March 31?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the title of the film released in February 2026 with the highest domestic gross between opening and March 31, 2026 according to "Daily Box Office Performance" figures found on the “Box Office” tab on this movie's The Numbers (https://www.the-numbers.com/) page.

In the event of an exact tie the film that comes first alphabetically will be considered the winner.

eventMetadata.context_description: Scream 7 dominates trader consensus at 99.6% implied probability as the highest domestically grossing February film by March 31, propelled by its franchise-best $55 million opening weekend and steady $120 million cumulative haul through week three, outpacing rivals amid positive word-of-mouth and Neve Campbell's return boosting buzz. Horror sequels historically thrive in post-holiday slots with strong multipliers, while competitors lag: I Can Only Imagine 2 at $25 million taps faith audiences but lacks broad appeal, Wuthering Heights struggles with niche period drama reception under $10 million, and GOAT's $8 million reflects limited U.S. traction for its Tamil action draw. Realistic upsets hinge on Scream 7's front-loaded drop from competition or viral surges for the others via streaming tie-ins, though current trajectories deem this improbable.

eventMetadata.context_description: Project Hail Mary dominates trader consensus at 95% implied probability as the highest-grossing March release through April 30, fueled by blockbuster tracking showing $100M+ opening weekend potential, Ryan Gosling's star power post-Barbie momentum, and the proven draw of Andy Weir's bestselling sci-fi IP under Phil Lord and Christopher Miller's direction. Realistic upsets hinge on Hail Mary's reviews tanking or unforeseen competition eroding its legs, though historical tentpole precedents make this improbable.

question: Will Jurgen Klopp be appointed as manager of Real Madrid?
description:
This market will resolve according to the person who is appointed as the next permanent manager of Real Madrid.

If no permanent manager is appointed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other." 

Appointments of 'interim,' 'caretaker,' or other non-permanent managers will not impact this market's resolution.

An announcement of a new permanent manager's appointment before this market's close date will immediately resolve this market to the corresponding option, regardless of when the announced appointment goes into effect.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Real Madrid; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Next Real Madrid manager?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the person who is appointed as the next permanent manager of Real Madrid.

If no permanent manager is appointed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other." 

Appointments of 'interim,' 'caretaker,' or other non-permanent managers will not impact this market's resolution.

An announcement of a new permanent manager's appointment before this market's close date will immediately resolve this market to the corresponding option, regardless of when the announced appointment goes into effect.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Real Madrid; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Real Madrid's next manager remains tightly contested, with no outcome exceeding 21% implied probability amid Carlo Ancelotti's contract extension through 2026 and no confirmed vacancy. Massimiliano Allegri and Jurgen Klopp lead at 21.0% each—Allegri's trophy-laden Juventus tenure and availability post-sacking appeal to Florentino Perez's preference for serial winners, while Klopp's high-pressing dynamism and recent Liverpool exit fuel speculation. Mikel Arteta (20.8%) draws buzz from his Arsenal success mirroring Zidane's legacy appeal, but club ties limit feasibility; Robert De Zerbi (18.0%) and Mauricio Pochettino (17.0%) reflect trader bets on Premier League innovators now free. Without leaks from the Bernabeu hierarchy or Ancelotti's age-65 succession signals, diverse candidate profiles—proven tacticians versus visionaries—keep the post-Ancelotti race fragmented.

Mikel Arteta (20.8%) draws buzz from his Arsenal success mirroring Zidane's legacy appeal, but club ties limit feasibility; Robert De Zerbi (18.0%) and Mauricio Pochettino (17.0%) reflect trader bets on Premier League innovators now free. Without leaks from the Bernabeu hierarchy or Ancelotti's age-65 succession signals, diverse candidate profiles—proven tacticians versus visionaries—keep the post-Ancelotti race fragmented.

question: Will Albania be in the top 3 at Eurovision 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the named country finishes Eurovision 2026 as one of the top 3 highest scoring candidates.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to finish as one of the top 3 highest scorers based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

events.title: Eurovision 2026: Top 3
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the named country finishes Eurovision 2026 as one of the top 3 highest scoring candidates.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to finish as one of the top 3 highest scorers based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket's Eurovision 2026 Top 3 market heavily favors perennial powerhouses Sweden (implied 45% for top finish), Ukraine (38%), and Italy (32%), reflecting their track records of strong entries, jury-televote splits, and diaspora voting blocs that have dominated recent contests. The primary driver remains uncertainty over the 2026 host—determined by May 2025's winner in Basel, Switzerland—which grants auto-qualification to the final and home-crowd momentum, as seen in past boosts for Sweden's 2012-13 streak. Recent national selection teases from Melodifestivalen and Vidbir fuel optimism for these frontrunners, while geopolitical tensions could amplify Ukraine's sympathy votes; watch 2025 semifinals for shifts in trader sentiment ahead of 2026's likely May event in the victor nation.
